title: neomarica capitellata
intro: Neomarica capitellata, also known as Aneilla or Camalote, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a perennial plant with sword-shaped leaves and orange-red flowers that bloom in the spring. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Neomarica capitellata can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties, which may also provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
